title: Archive: EgoVLA / ViewVLA Standalone Track
description: Archive reason: EgoVLA already covers the core egocentric-human-video-to-robot-VLA topic; the actionable leftovers now live in UMI ego/exo and cross-embodiment Survey cards.
summary: Standalone EgoVLA/ViewVLA is archived to avoid duplicating active UMI and cross-embodiment work. Keep the paper as a reference for human-video pretraining, MANO/wrist action spaces, IK retargeting, and simulated humanoid manipulation evaluation.

details.text: EgoVLA directly studies training VLA models from egocentric human videos, predicting wrist/hand actions, retargeting via IK, and fine-tuning with robot demos. That completes the generic standalone topic; our useful delta is how to reuse the idea in UMI fixed-exo/ego generation and same-task HOI retargeting.

details: Moved-out live work: UMI / Image-Layered World Model owns ego/exo generation; Cross-Embodiment Representations owns the shared action/state representation decision; Diverse Same-Task Dataset owns collection and retargeting evidence.
Archive decision: do not keep a separate Survey card unless a new ViewVLA-style paper leaves a concrete first experiment not covered by those existing cards.
risks_decisions: Reason type: externally covered topic plus internal duplication with active Survey/Research cards.
Reopen condition: a new ego/exo method creates a measurable experiment that is not UMI world modeling and not cross-embodiment representation selection.
